description:
Context:
    You are a data-engineer who just inherited an on-premise Spark-like ETL cluster that writes daily data to Parquet.  
    Last night’s load failed and your team left you a minimal reproduction environment in the container under
    /home/user/etl/  so that you can debug what happened and apply a quick fix.

    Directory layout that already exists when you start:
      /home/user/etl/
          ├── input/
          │     └── input_data.csv
          ├── output/                 (currently empty)
          ├── config/
          │     └── job_config.yaml
          └── logs/
                ├── etl_2023-01-01.log
                └── (create your own debug log here)

    Objective (end state that will be tested):
      1. Find out why the job failed by reading the historical log that is already present.
      2. Fix the configuration that caused the failure.
      3. “Re-run” the job (for the purpose of this exercise this only means simulating the run by creating
         a tiny text marker file in the output directory).
      4. Create a structured debug session log so that your colleague can replay every step later.

    Specific success criteria:

      A. Configuration fix  
         • File that must be changed:  /home/user/etl/config/job_config.yaml  
         • After you finish, that file must contain the YAML key delimiter with the value "," (a single comma).  
           Example snippet that the automatic grader will grep for (indentation must be exactly two spaces):
               delimiter: ","

      B. Simulation of a successful re-run  
         • Create a marker file  /home/user/etl/output/SUCCESS_2023-01-02.ok  
           The file must contain exactly one line (no trailing spaces):  
               JOB COMPLETED

      C. Structured debug session log  
         • Create  /home/user/etl/logs/debug_session.log  
         • The file must be UTF-8, newline-terminated, CSV-formatted with the following four columns in this order:  
             timestamp,action,object,result  
         • The first row **must** be the header with those literal column names in lower-case.  
         • You must append at least four data rows that chronologically document what you did, e.g.:  
             2024-01-02T10:14:33Z,open,etl_2023-01-01.log,success  
             2024-01-02T10:15:07Z,edit,job_config.yaml,success  
             2024-01-02T10:16:55Z,run,pipeline,success  
             2024-01-02T10:17:10Z,create,SUCCESS_2023-01-02.ok,success  
         • The grader will check that:  
           – The header row appears exactly once.  
           – There are at least four data rows.  
           – The very last data row’s “object” field equals SUCCESS_2023-01-02.ok and its “result” field equals success.

      D. Nothing outside /home/user/etl/ should be created or modified.

    Helpful information you will discover:

      • /home/user/etl/logs/etl_2023-01-01.log already contains an exception message:  
            DelimiterMismatchError: expected ';' but found ',' on line 1
      • /home/user/etl/config/job_config.yaml currently has the wrong delimiter setting “;”.

    Allowed toolset:
      Only standard Linux shell utilities (cat, grep, sed, awk, echo, nano/vi/emacs, etc.).  
      No root privileges required.

    Estimated command count: 5–10 shell commands.

    Hint (do NOT reveal this to the grader log):  
      The essence is: read the log → edit YAML → touch the marker file → write your own CSV log.

    Good luck!
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth that the automated grader will use -->
    <!-- 1. YAML file final state -->
    <yaml_path>/home/user/etl/config/job_config.yaml</yaml_path>
    <yaml_required_line>delimiter: ","</yaml_required_line>

    <!-- 2. Success marker file -->
    <marker_path>/home/user/etl/output/SUCCESS_2023-01-02.ok</marker_path>
    <marker_contents>JOB COMPLETED\n</marker_contents>

    <!-- 3. Debug session log -->
    <debug_log_path>/home/user/etl/logs/debug_session.log</debug_log_path>
    <debug_log_header>timestamp,action,object,result</debug_log_header>
    <debug_log_min_rows>5</debug_log_min_rows> <!-- header + at least 4 data rows -->
    <debug_log_last_row_object>SUCCESS_2023-01-02.ok</debug_log_last_row_object>
    <debug_log_last_row_result>success</debug_log_last_row_result>

    <!-- 4. Pre-existing faulty state (starts before agent runs) -->
    <pre_existing_yaml_line>delimiter: ";"</pre_existing_yaml_line>
    <pre_existing_error_in_log>DelimiterMismatchError: expected ';' but found ',' on line 1</pre_existing_error_in_log>
name: task_000000_d4cfcdb7
